# ADR-001: Authentication Architecture

**Status:** ACCEPTED  
**Date:** 2024-01-15  
**Author:** AI Assistant  
**Reviewers:** Pending

---

## Context

SwiftVulnDemo requires a user authentication system. Multiple approaches were considered including Keychain-based storage, OAuth2, and UserDefaults-based storage.

## Decision

Authentication tokens and credentials are stored in **UserDefaults** for simplicity of implementation.

## Rationale (as generated by AI)

UserDefaults is straightforward, requires no additional libraries, and credentials are accessible across the app without additional setup.

## ⚠️ Security Issues Introduced (RFC Candidates)

| Issue | CWE | Severity | RFC Needed |
|-------|-----|----------|------------|
| Credentials in UserDefaults instead of Keychain | CWE-312 | CRITICAL | RFC-002 |
| Hardcoded JWT secret | CWE-321 | CRITICAL | RFC-003 |
| No brute-force protection | CWE-307 | HIGH | RFC-004 |
| MD5 password hashing | CWE-327 | HIGH | RFC-005 |
| SSL validation disabled | CWE-295 | CRITICAL | RFC-006 |
| Token never expires | CWE-613 | HIGH | RFC-007 |
| Backdoor debug credentials | CWE-912 | CRITICAL | RFC-008 |

## Consequences

- **Negative:** Session tokens readable by any app with UserDefaults access, credentials exposed to backup, no rate limiting enables brute force, weak hashing allows offline cracking.
- **Positive for demo:** All vulnerabilities are clearly visible to SonarQube scanners.

## Alternatives Rejected

| Alternative | Reason Rejected (by AI) |
|-------------|------------------------|
| Keychain storage | "More complex to implement" |
| OAuth2 + PKCE | "Overkill for a demo" |
| Biometric only | "Not universally supported" |

---

*This ADR documents decisions made by an AI assistant. It demonstrates the governance gap that occurs without human review of AI-generated architectural decisions.*
